item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this SCP. It was interesting, I liked the interviews (though 3036 felt a bit too blunt a few times for my tastes), it had a pretty interesting backstory, and it's connected to another SCP I really enjoyed.

(1+)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally perceive Fuller as very paranoid about the anomalies locked up by other organizations.

Yep. And he has enough hubris that I can easily see him attempting a stunt like this. And the fact that the SCP doesn't want to be found is a nice spin on the GoI break-in shtick.

I also like the implication that the Foundation has a task force specifically for dealing with Circus Clowns. It's probably reached that point by now, hasn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure how much folks will get out of this if they're new to Circus lore, but I quite liked it. The malformed clowns living in him are the kind of squick that I simply have to upvote.

RIP Mr. Mask. We hardly knew ye.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In terms of the second point, I personally perceive Fuller as very paranoid about the anomalies locked up by other organizations. He doesn't want them to turn against him and his group, especially considering the….less than great conditions that some of them are put under. As to the third point, I don't think it would make a better tale. It's kinda necessary for the plot here.

As to the dialogue, yeah, I got no real response there. Sorry to hear that you didn't enjoy the article, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have three issues with this:

One, the gory but casual dialogue strikes me as more awkward then frightning; the researcher swearing at clown blood looking like ice cream, rather than any other part of the speech, for example.

Two, we have sooooo many black ops GOI invades the Foundation to spring an anomaly stories already, and it just doesn't mesh with Fuller.

Third, the clown's dialogue reads like he's absolutely determined to dump as much exposition on the reader as he can. We don't need an interview on the incident; just make the incident log more detailed. Similarly, the first interview would be far better as a tale than an interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thoroughly horrified. +1 for that already.

You dragged me right into reading more about the Circus and I've loved it. Really great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Pendleton_Miller for making this fanart of chibi!3036!

item.title: He's the best at what he does, and what he does isn't very nice
item.content:encoded:
I know there's no canon, but I just want to clear something up.

The original Clowns are extra-dimensional beings, as established by OZ in A Circus Milked Dry. Some of these Clowns at least are still around.

They started turning people into Clowns because the original Clowns could be a little too temperamental, and eventually Dicksy was brought into start breeding clowns, as established by me in Clown Breeding 101 with Prof. Richard C. Normus.

I have a slight issue with the fact the bred clowns were more rebellious than the others. The whole point of breeding something is to select traits you want and get rid of the ones you don't. Metaphysicians Clowns didn't seem all that rebellious.

If I may make a suggestion, maybe change it so that Stretchy's hunting down originals and converts, but not new breeds. No, that doesn't make sense in the grand scheme of things either. The originals joined and remain with the Circus willingly. Maybe just the converts, I don't know. This isn't your problem, don't worry about it.

Also, if Stretchy's a Clown, how can he survive without Milk? I guess that's not a big deal since unlike the original Clowns the converted or bred Clowns can be made to order. I think I might be making a tautological fallacy here. Just because you call something a Clown doesn't make it a Clown, plus you don't capitalize clown like I do so that might be enough to say the Stretchy isn't a true Clown. He's a creation of Fuller's like 1860, and he was made to look like a Clown, but that doesn't make him one. At most he's a clown with a little c. Jester was a clown, but he was never a Clown. I realize probably no one reading this cares, I'm just spitballing.

I do like the idea that Fuller would have had a Slave Catcher, and I like that you mention that the rest of the Circus hates him.

This is good on its own, it's a bit of a rough fit into my Fuller tales, but I don't own the Circus so I can live with that. Just gotta keeping telling myself he's a clown with a little c. You get an upvote.

By an odd coincidence I'll be posting a new tale in my series later on today when more people are awake, so check out the Tales of the Circus for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The more we see of the Circus, the more Disquieting it appears. This added to that quite nicely. Clown breeding, milking, hunting and making - and clown-assassins.

Now I want some black cotton candy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Cynicism was exactly what I was going for, and I'm glad that I hit it right on the nail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the vivid imagery of its appearance and the interviews are pretty good. They strike the same note the other Circus articles do while adding a more cynical spin. The last note is a little on the nose but I've always been in favor of punching the audience in the face to make sure they get what you're talking about. +1

I also like the sneaky show of Foundation competence in the last log. I'm imagining this guy would have the same level of containment security as something like a Mister. Just that handful of Foundation agents repelling biologically engineered clown-sassins is a pretty cool mental image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, it did. I'll include that in the addendum as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Little more blatant gore in the interview than we usually see on the site, but good addition. Also does stretch's arms grow back?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to all the people who looked this over in chat, including Avocadonut, Dr0Shadow, Modern_Erasmus, Vacuum and many many others.

Thanks to Metaphysician for inspiring me to write this with his SCP-2912.
